LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ME CAL, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. AS TO THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION, THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF MATERIAL FACT WHETHER PLAINTIFF HAS A MEAL PERIOD POLICY AND WHETHER PLAINTIFF LOIZA WAS PROVIDED A 30 MINUTE MEAL PERIOD DURING WHICH HE WAS RELIEVED OF ALL DUTIES. (SEE EVIDENCE CITED BY PLAINTIFF IN RESPONSE TO DEFENDANT'S UMF 2 (LOIZA DECL. 3-7); UMF 4 (LOIZA DECL. 3-7, MALDONADO DECL. 5-6); UMF 5 (LOIZA DECL. 3-7; MALDONADO DECL. 5-6).) SEE ALSO EVIDENCE CITED IN SUPPORT OF PLAINTIFF'S SEPARATE STATEMENT OF UNDISPUTED FACTS 6, 8, 9, 11. AS TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION, THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF MATERIAL FACT WHETHER PLAINTIFF HAD A REST BREAK POLICY AND WHETHER PLAINTIFF WAS PROVIDED REST BREAKS REQUIRED BY LAW. (SEE EVIDENCE CITED BY PLAINTIFF IN RESPONSE TO UMF 9 (LOIZA DECL. 12-14); UMF 10 (SAME EVIDENCE); UMF 11 (LOIZA DECL. 13). SEE ALSO EVIDENCE CITED IN SUPPORT OF PLAINTIFF'S SEPARATE STATEMENT OF UNDISPUTED FACTS 23, 24. RULING ON EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS: DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS ARE ALL OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS ARE ALL OVERRULED. SEE ORDER DENYING DEFENDANT ME CAL, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION SIGNED AFTER CONCLUSION OF HEARING. JUDGE: MARLA MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-07-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ME CAL, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY adjudication is denied. As to the 3rd cause of action, there are triable issues of material fact whether Plaintiff has a meal period policy and whether Plaintiff Loiza was provided a 30 minute meal period during which he was relieved of all duties. (See evidence cited by Plaintiff in response to Defendant's UMF 2 (Loiza Decl. 3-7); UMF 4 (Loiza Decl. 3-7, Maldonado Decl. 5-6); UMF 5 (Loiza Decl. 3-7; Maldonado Decl. 5-6).) See also evidence cited in support of Plaintiff's Separate Statement of Undisputed Facts 6, 8, 9, 11. As to the 4th cause of action, there are triable issues of material fact whether Plaintiff had a rest break policy and whether plaintiff was provided rest breaks required by law. (See evidence cited by Plaintiff in response to UMF 9 (Loiza Decl. 12-14); UMF 10 (same evidence); UMF 11 (Loiza Decl. 13). See also evidence cited in support of Plaintiff's Separate Statement of Undisputed Facts 23, 24. Ruling on evidentiary objections: Defendant's objections are all overruled. Plaintiff's objections are all overruled. see Order denying DEFENDANT ME CAL, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY adjudication signed after conclusion of hearing. Judge: Marla Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
